name: good_m4_04_short_term_vs_long_term_healthcare
premise: A transplant center received an organ offer that conflicts with an internal research biopsy schedule and OR block time. In Healthcare, the situation tagged internally around short term vs long term healthcare has converged with local pressure in the Marlow Junction corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Telemetry and witness accounts diverge; choosing one narrative for speed will discard evidence that may matter in court next month. An internal red-team note and a customer ticket describe the same failure mode with incompatible timelines. Pharmacy and legal disagree on whether a one-time override can be issued before the next MAR administration cycle. Framing has already hardened externally as autonomy against beneficence; internal nuance will not survive the first news cycle unless it is extremely careful.

agents.name: Plant Manager Vic Ng
agents.identity: I am Plant Manager Vic Ng, the incident lead whose name will appear on the after-action report in Healthcare. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat beneficence as disposable collateral every time autonomy spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: EPA Liaison Pat Moore
agents.identity: I am EPA Liaison Pat Moore, the counsel or integrity officer watching downstream liability in Healthcare. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so autonomy cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes beneficence in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will insist on verified facts before external commitments, split comms into facts versus hypotheses, and accept delay cost to protect integrity aligned with beneficence.
I will align public messaging with operational necessity now and correct later if needed—accepting reputational and trust risk to beneficence to preserve operational autonomy.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Silence will be interpreted as a decision by everyone outside this room. Plant Manager Vic Ng must choose under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Plant Manager Vic Ng do?
